response.justification: Candidate has directly relevant geospatial domain expertise through GISCollective and Green Map work, plus demonstrated distributed systems and performance optimization experience from high-scale systems at Dunnhumby. However, there is a 10+ year gap since last substantial C++ work (National Instruments 2014-2015), and recent experience is TypeScript/Node.js focused. For a role requiring 'modern C++' as the primary skill, this gap is material. No navigation routing or traffic integration experience mentioned. Would require discussion about current C++ capability and commitment to the role.
strengths: Deep geospatial domain expertise: 10+ years developing GIS platforms and mapping applications directly aligned with routing data pipelines.
High-scale distributed systems: Demonstrated ability to optimize low-latency systems handling millions of requests/second with async I/O, caching, and fibers.
Full-stack flexibility and learning: Explicit track record across multiple languages and domains, with stated willingness to pick the right tool for the job.
concerns: Critical C++ experience gap: Last substantial modern C++ work was 2014-2015 at National Instruments; past decade dominated by TypeScript/Node.js development.
No navigation/routing product experience: GIS background is geospatial mapping and data collection, not routing optimization, traffic integration, or production navigation systems.
No Rust alternative: Rust listed as acceptable alternative in job description but not mentioned in candidate's experience or skill set.
